title: rapanea affinis
intro: Rapanea affinis, also known as the yellow wood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrsinaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with its leaves and bark exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to be useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Rapanea affinis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil for medicinal purposes, with some herbal practitioners also using it in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
